explanation: The provided code implements an assembler for a simplified assembly language. It reads an input .asm file, removes whitespace and comments, and constructs a symbol table for labels. It processes each command, categorizing it as A-instruction, C-instruction, or label, and then translates these commands into their corresponding binary format, outputting the results to a .hack file. Key functions include translation of destination, computation, and jump components of C-instructions, as well as handling of label addresses.
subject: Building Computing Systems - HW 6
summary: This project is an assembler program that translates assembly language (.asm) into machine-readable code (.hack). It processes the input assembly code to eliminate unnecessary whitespace and comments, and generates a binary representation of the commands.
